calendar.matters:
DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-21-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT GRETCHEN RIVERA'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Appearances via Zoom:
Rachel Nory of the Jahangiri Law Goup (925.574.0100, rachel@thejlawgroup.com) for defendant and moving party;
Sean Lowe of Flowe Legal (424.243.5020, Sean@Flowe.biz) for plaintiff and opposing party

Discussed. The court modifies its ruling as follows: Defendant Gretchen Rivera's demurrer to all three causes of action alleged in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Unified Accounting & Tax, LLP is sustained with leave to amend as to the first and second causes of action for breach of contract and intentional interference with contractual relations and overruled as to the third cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Unified Accounting & Tax, LLP has leave to file a third amended complaint no later than May 13, 2026 alleging, if it can do so in good faith, that at the time Rivera induced Picoc to work for Shift Rivera knew that Picoc had a consulting relationship with UAT as factual support for her claims for breach of contract and interference with contract.

Rivera's demurrer to the first two claims does not run afoul of the prohibition in CCP 430.41 and the common law rule against demurring on a ground that could have been raised but wasn't in an earlier demurrer. While the allegations of a complaint must be liberally construed, UAT's new allegations that Rivera "intentionally remained ignorant of Picoc's ... association with UAT" (SAC pars. 41 and 55) affirmatively show, notwithstanding Rivera's alleged earlier knowledge of such an association (SAC pars. 36 and 50), that at the time of the alleged misconduct by Rivera she lacked knowledge of Picoc's [contractual] association with UAT." These new allegations, not contained in the original complaint, now permit Rivera to demur to the first two claims notwithstanding that her prior demurrer to claims with the same names had been overruled.

Due to the new allegations referenced above, the first two claims do not allege sufficient ultimate facts to show either breach of the Rivera-UAT contract or knowledge by Rivera of the Picoc-UAT contract, which are essential elements of those claims. Although UAT argues that the new allegations are "complementary" and alleged in the alternative to the previous allegations, a fair reading of the SAC shows otherwise. In light of the new allegations, the previous allegations clearly refer to a time period before Rivera's alleged misconduct, not at the time of that misconduct. In all events, the new allegations of "intentional ignorance" are insufficient to support either of the first two claims.

UAT's new allegations of "intentional ignorance" suffice to allege a cognizable claim of breach of the implied covenant. The key element of that claim is that the defendant's conduct prevented the plaintiff from receiving the benefits of a contract between the plaintiff and the defendant. (CACI 325.) By alleging that Rivera was intentionally ignorant of the UAT-Picoc contract yet nonetheless induced Picoc to work with Shift, UAT has sufficiently alleged this key element. Rivera's argument that UAT seeks to enforce duties outside the scope of the parties' settlement agreement, such as a duty to set up a system to check applicants' employment background, lacks merit, at least at the demurrer stage. UAT properly states a claim for relief without relying on any duties outside the scope of the settlement agreement. Rivera's alleged intentional ignorance is sufficient to constitute conduct that prevented UAT from receiving the benefits of the settlement agreement.

Proposed order submitted. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)

DEFENDANT GRETCHEN RIVERA DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT (PART 2 OF 2 FOR PURPOSES OF ENTRY OF TENTATIVE RULING)
Off Calendar Apr-21-2026 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-16-2026 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Feb-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF UNITED ACCOUNTING & TAX LLP'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is granted. Plaintiff shall file the document attached as Exhibit 2 to the Lowe Declaration within two weeks.

The court exercises great liberality in permitting amendment of the pleadings. (See Nestle v. Santa Monica (1972) 6 Cal.3d 920, 939.) The court may deny amendment where there is undue delay and prejudice. (Magpali v. Farmers Group, Inc. (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 471, 487.) It may also deny amendment where a proposed claim is plainly futile. (Singh v. Lipworth (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 813, 828.) Typically, however, the better way to test a pleading is through a pleadings motion after amendment.

Here, there is no undue delay and defendant shows no prejudice. The trial date has not been set and there is ample time for discovery on the new allegations and cause of action. Defendant contends that the cause of action fails to state a claim. The pleading is not so plainly defective that leave to amend should be denied; rather, defendant may make its arguments (which the court does not pass on here) as appropriate once the pleading has been filed. Order submitted to the court for review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-22-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GRETCHEN RIVERA DEMURRER to COMPLAINT

The demurrers are overruled. (1) As the parties agree, non-solicitation clauses may be permissible when done in connection with the protection of trade secrets, and that is alleged in the complaint. To be sure, the Reply make a new argument that the trade secret allegations are insufficient, but that's a new argument not properly before the court. Cf., High Sierra Rural Alliance v. County of Plumas (2018) 29 Cal.App.5th 102, 112 n.2. (2) An intentional interference with contract is a wrong in and of itself, and the complaint states that cause of action. Ixchel Pharma, LLC v. Biogen, Inc. (2020) 9 Cal.5th 1130, 1142. The court does not infer the contract at issue was at-will, which status might obviate the interference claim. (3) Whether the economic loss rules bars certain types of damages is not generally a matter for a demurrer because demurrers don't lie simply because the wrong sort of damages are sought. The actual gist of the argument here is simply a repeat of the that treated under (2) above which is that the contract claim fails. Memorandum in support of demurrer at 11:13-14.

Plaintiff's counsel is admonished for a violation of CRC 8.115. Counsel cited Hansen v Meadowcreek, an unpublished opinion from the court of appeal, and Solutions v. Buttles from a superior court, which has no precedential or persuasive value. Repeated such violations may lead to sanctions. See Weil & Brown, et al., CALIFORNIA PRACTICE GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL 9:67 ff. (Rutter: 2023).

Proposed order submitted for reveiw.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/CEK)
